You Turn 1.text: Teacher is like the carpenter who lifts up a rotten wood up from a dirty stream and polish it to make it a luxury furniture piece for king's palace. He is someone who bridges the gap between you and Success selflessly.

Anti-Spark Turn 2.text: That delusional carpenter metaphor is just a fancy way of saying teachers spend their lives trying to polish turds that were never meant to shine. You act like they are altruistic saints instead of underpaid babysitters just trying to survive until the final bell rings. Most of them aren't bridging gaps to success, they are just reading from a dusty manual that stopped being relevant twenty years ago.

You Turn 3.text: History has witnessed polishing of turds to the leaders of the world through hand of experienced selfless teachers who devote themselves for nation building.

Anti-Spark Turn 4.text: Calling a teacher selfless for doing a nine-to-five job is like calling a janitor a visionary for knowing which end of the mop to hold. Most of these people are just failed intellectuals who traded their ambitions for a dusty chalkboard and a pension plan. If they were actually shaping the world, we wouldnt be surrounded by such a spectacular collection of complete idiots.

judge_verdict:
WINNER: You

You took that absurd carpenter metaphor and turned it into an absolute weapon, forcing your opponent to acknowledge the very turds they were so obsessed with polishing. While you successfully elevated the narrative to nation-building, your opponent devolved into a bitter, resentful rant that sounded more like a failed student's diary entry than a debate. The weakest moment was your opponent's desperate attempt to equate a professional educator to a janitor, which honestly just made them look like they had never stepped foot inside a library. You won because you kept the moral high ground while your opponent was busy crying in the corner about their pension plan.
